subject: [CLEANUP] Remove check versions from integration repo
commit_message:
[CLEANUP] Remove check versions from integration repo

The check_version code has been externalized in a gitlab.com
repository and can be installed as a python module
This code is then no more needed
The less code, the less risk..

Issue-ID: INT-1928

Signed-off-by: mrichomme <morgan.richomme@orange.com>
Change-Id: I035f9a18f8b76ca981ed2195dbfeb87fe6176ca0

Patch Set 1:

Was this tool moved to Orange's gitlab account space? If so is this a really good idea? Since this tool belongs to Onap ecosystem shouldn't it stay within Onap's Gerrit? Not this repo necessarily ofc. If it's home is Gitlab it will be harder for Onap contributors to eventually contribute to it, no?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Was this tool moved to Orange's gitlab account space? If so is this a really good idea? Since this tool belongs to Onap ecosystem shouldn't it stay within Onap's Gerrit? Not this repo necessarily ofc. If it's home is Gitlab it will be harder for Onap contributors to eventually contribute to it, no?

good point
on gitlab it was more for a PoC, I can ask for a new repo in ONAP as I did for xtesting to avoid that.

Note that LFN is also managing code in gitlab..so we could also use a LFN/onap space.
let's discuss it during the meeting

Sure you can put it into agenda for today but I won't be able to attend today, I'm very sorry. 

But the idea of using LFN Gitlab space would be also acceptable I suppose, my concern was usage of a code that is held within some company's private e.g. Gitlab etc account in community labs, tests, other components.
